---
title: "Object-Oriented Python"
description: "Use classes when data and behavior belong together, while avoiding unnecessary complexity."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [object-oriented-python]
order: 410
---

## Why this chapter matters

Use classes when data and behavior belong together, while avoiding unnecessary complexity.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.callout-note title="Guiding questions"}
- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Object-Oriented Basics](oop-basics.qmd) — Define classes, create objects, store attributes, write methods, and use self.
- [Dataclasses](dataclasses.qmd) — Create simple data-focused classes with less boilerplate.
- [Object-Oriented Design](oop-design.qmd) — Choose composition, inheritance, or simpler functions based on the relationship you need.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "object-oriented-python-overview",
  "title": "Object-Oriented Python overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Object-Oriented Python chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Use classes when data and behavior belong together, while avoiding unnecessary complexity.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Use classes when data and behavior belong together, while avoiding unnecessary complexity."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
